proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of the natural direct effect through multiple sequential mediators with bounded mediator-outcome confounding via negative-control proxies, when only the marginal mediator distributions are observed and the joint mediator dependency structure is partially specified
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains ThmSmith/doc/API.md §3: ExactID and PartialID catalogues are empty at seed time, with no in-catalogue multi-mediator proxy-bound proposal. AutoID promoted front-door material is single-mediator exact identification, while AutoID partial-ID proxy and Balke--Pearl material supplies background machinery rather than this marginal-mediator coupling/proxy relaxation kernel.","published_axis":"Closest published comparators are GabrielSachsSjolander2023 for sharp two-binary-mediator decomposition bounds, GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 for proxy partial identification without completeness, and GhassamiYangShpitserTchetgen2025 for hidden-mediator proximal identification. The revised gap is that none studies bounded proxy-confounding plus marginal-only mediator dependence where the coordinatewise union of fixed-q proxy intervals creates a rectangular relaxation strictly wider than the joint LP endpoint."}
seed_list: Prior work GabrielSachsSjolander2023 would imply that two-binary-mediator sharp bounds can be organized once the mediator joint law is fixed. Our seed conjectures that with only mediator marginals, negative-control proxy bridge equations force the PNDE optimum to an interior mediator-coupling face rather than a Fréchet endpoint.

Prior work GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 would predict that proxy partial-ID bounds can be applied to a target after replacing the target by the relevant counterfactual mean. Our seed conjectures that PNDE weighting by an unobserved mediator coupling makes the proxy-bound problem non-rectangular because the same bridge-null direction is pasted across mediator cells.

Prior work DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 establishes proximal mediation identification when bridge conditions identify the natural-effect functionals. Our seed conjectures that when completeness fails and mediator joint dependence is only partially specified, the remaining unidentified direction is not a bridge function alone but a coupled bridge-copula coordinate.

Prior work VansteelandtDaniel2017 would imply that multiple-mediator interventional effects can be defined through draws from a mediator-vector distribution. Our seed conjectures that when only mediator marginals are available, the dependence term becomes a sharp partial-ID coordinate whose active constraint can come from outcome proxies rather than mediator data.

Prior work XiaChan2022 assumes copula-compatible nuisance modeling for multiple natural mediation effects. Our seed conjectures that in partial identification the copula is not merely a nuisance compatibility device; proxy bridge constraints can turn a copula interior point into the sharp PNDE extremum.

Prior work DingVanderWeele2016 establishes sharp sensitivity bounds for single-mediator natural effects under unmeasured mediator-outcome confounding. Our seed conjectures that with two sequential mediators, the same confounding budget can tighten one cell and widen another through mediator dependence, creating a bound not obtainable by tensoring single-mediator bounds.

Prior work MilesKankiMeloniTchetgen2017 gives partial identification for a binary-mediator natural indirect effect when cross-world/no-induced-confounding assumptions fail. Our seed conjectures that two binary mediators add a dependence coordinate whose sharp contribution can be signed by negative-control proxy equations.

Prior work DanielDeStavolaCousensVansteelandt2015 assumes that joint multiple-mediator behavior matters for valid mediation decomposition. Our seed conjectures that if the joint mediator law is unobserved but partially specified, even the joint mediated effect has a smallest binary counterexample to endpoint-only Fréchet optimization.

Prior work VanderWeeleVansteelandt2014 shows multiple-mediator methods can be robust to unmeasured common causes of mediators in some joint-effect settings. Our seed conjectures that this robustness fails for marginal-only mediator data because the unmeasured common cause becomes an identified-set coordinate rather than a nuisance canceled by joint observation.

Prior work HanLuoZhangGu2026 would imply that observing part of mediator-endpoint confounding narrows direct/indirect-effect bounds by stratification. Our seed conjectures that negative-control proxy information is qualitatively different: it can couple response cells across unobserved mediator-dependence strata and produce interior active faces.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work GabrielSachsSjolander2023 would imply that two-binary-mediator sharp bounds can be organized once the mediator joint law is fixed. Our seed conjectures that with only mediator marginals, negative-control proxy bridge equations force the PNDE optimum to an interior mediator-coupling face rather than a Fréchet endpoint.

anchor_paper.author: Gabriel, Sachs, Sjolander
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
anchor_paper.claim: Derives sharp bounds for decomposition effects with two binary mediators and notes separately sharp decomposition bounds usually do not combine sharply.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern, not blocked: the seed predicts a new active-face phenomenon caused by missing joint mediator dependence plus proxy equations, not merely a weaker assumption.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from GabrielSachsSjolander2023 because they already solve sharp two-binary-mediator bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their LP fixes or observes the relevant joint mediator structure and has no shared negative-control bridge-envelope constraints; the proposed interior-coupling face is outside their setup.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 would predict that proxy partial-ID bounds can be applied to a target after replacing the target by the relevant counterfactual mean. Our seed conjectures that PNDE weighting by an unobserved mediator coupling makes the proxy-bound problem non-rectangular because the same bridge-null direction is pasted across mediator cells.

anchor_paper.author: Ghassami, Shpitser, Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Establishes proxy-based partial-identification bounds without completeness for causal effects and related hidden-mediator settings.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target-variation pattern, not blocked: the new target reveals a hidden identification gap because the proxy target is weighted by an unobserved mediator coupling.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 because their proxy partial-ID theorem already handles nonunique bridges.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their bounds do not include a natural-effect sum over an unobserved joint mediator distribution with shared mediator-cell bridge constraints.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 establishes proximal mediation identification when bridge conditions identify the natural-effect functionals. Our seed conjectures that when completeness fails and mediator joint dependence is only partially specified, the remaining unidentified direction is not a bridge function alone but a coupled bridge-copula coordinate.

anchor_paper.author: Dukes, Shpitser, Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Gives proximal identification results for natural direct and indirect effects in the presence of unmeasured confounding.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakening-completeness pattern, not blocked: the proposed mechanism is a coupled bridge-copula unidentified coordinate rather than a broader hypothesis class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from proximal mediation because failure of completeness just yields partial identification over bridge functions.
seed_details.rebuttal: The mediator joint distribution is also latent here, so the unidentified coordinate changes the target weights rather than only the bridge response.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work VansteelandtDaniel2017 would imply that multiple-mediator interventional effects can be defined through draws from a mediator-vector distribution. Our seed conjectures that when only mediator marginals are available, the dependence term becomes a sharp partial-ID coordinate whose active constraint can come from outcome proxies rather than mediator data.

anchor_paper.author: Vansteelandt and Daniel
anchor_paper.claim: Defines interventional effects for mediation analysis with multiple mediators using mediator-vector intervention distributions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assignment/target variation pattern, not blocked: missing vector distribution exposes a new active constraint source from proxies.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just the obvious fact that marginal mediator laws do not identify a joint intervention distribution.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed is not nonidentification alone; it predicts a sharp interior active face caused by proxy equations.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work XiaChan2022 assumes copula-compatible nuisance modeling for multiple natural mediation effects. Our seed conjectures that in partial identification the copula is not merely a nuisance compatibility device; proxy bridge constraints can turn a copula interior point into the sharp PNDE extremum.

anchor_paper.author: Xia and Chan
anchor_paper.claim: Decomposes and estimates natural mediation effects with multiple mediators using compatible nuisance models, including copula-based reparameterization.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism-decomposition pattern, not blocked: it changes the role of the copula from nuisance compatibility to target-sharpness geometry.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from copula sensitivity analysis for multiple mediators.
seed_details.rebuttal: Existing copula work models or estimates a compatible joint law; it does not give sharp proxy-constrained bounds when only marginal mediator laws are observed.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DingVanderWeele2016 establishes sharp sensitivity bounds for single-mediator natural effects under unmeasured mediator-outcome confounding. Our seed conjectures that with two sequential mediators, the same confounding budget can tighten one cell and widen another through mediator dependence, creating a bound not obtainable by tensoring single-mediator bounds.

anchor_paper.author: Ding and VanderWeele
anchor_paper.claim: Gives sharp sensitivity bounds for mediation under unmeasured mediator-outcome confounding without parametric assumptions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extension from one to two mediators, not blocked: the asserted phenomenon is a tensorization failure/sign change in active cells.
seed_details.referee_objection: This follows by applying the single-mediator sensitivity bound to each mediator cell.
seed_details.rebuttal: Cellwise tensoring ignores the shared no-treatment mediator coupling and can report unattainable joint PNDE extrema.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MilesKankiMeloniTchetgen2017 gives partial identification for a binary-mediator natural indirect effect when cross-world/no-induced-confounding assumptions fail. Our seed conjectures that two binary mediators add a dependence coordinate whose sharp contribution can be signed by negative-control proxy equations.

anchor_paper.author: Miles, Kanki, Meloni, Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Studies partial identification of the natural indirect effect for a binary mediator when standard mediation assumptions fail.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extension to two mediators, not blocked: it predicts a proxy-signed dependence coordinate absent from the single-mediator problem.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just the two-mediator version of binary-mediator partial identification.
seed_details.rebuttal: A single binary mediator has no unobserved mediator copula coordinate, so the proposed active-face issue cannot occur there.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DanielDeStavolaCousensVansteelandt2015 assumes that joint multiple-mediator behavior matters for valid mediation decomposition. Our seed conjectures that if the joint mediator law is unobserved but partially specified, even the joint mediated effect has a smallest binary counterexample to endpoint-only Fréchet optimization.

anchor_paper.author: Daniel, De Stavola, Cousens, Vansteelandt
anchor_paper.claim: Shows multiple-mediator causal mediation requires joint reasoning and practical assumptions beyond one-at-a-time analysis.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/counterexample-search pattern, not blocked: the seed asks for smallest endpoint-failure witness, not a generic extension.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate because Fréchet bounds for missing joint distributions are classical.
seed_details.rebuttal: Classical Fréchet bounds do not include shared negative-control proxy equations that can move the optimum away from endpoints.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work VanderWeeleVansteelandt2014 shows multiple-mediator methods can be robust to unmeasured common causes of mediators in some joint-effect settings. Our seed conjectures that this robustness fails for marginal-only mediator data because the unmeasured common cause becomes an identified-set coordinate rather than a nuisance canceled by joint observation.

anchor_paper.author: VanderWeele and Vansteelandt
anchor_paper.claim: Extends mediation analysis to multiple mediators and characterizes joint mediated effects under interactions and common causes of mediators.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assumption-inversion pattern, not blocked: the named surprising mechanism is conversion of mediator common-cause dependence into a sharp-bound coordinate.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is merely weakening the joint mediator observation requirement.
seed_details.rebuttal: The claimed phenomenon is not wider bounds but a non-rectangular proxy-coupling geometry.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work HanLuoZhangGu2026 would imply that observing part of mediator-endpoint confounding narrows direct/indirect-effect bounds by stratification. Our seed conjectures that negative-control proxy information is qualitatively different: it can couple response cells across unobserved mediator-dependence strata and produce interior active faces.

anchor_paper.author: Han, Luo, Zhang, Gu
anchor_paper.claim: Derives bounds for direct and indirect effects when part of mediator-endpoint confounding is observed.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism-decomposition pattern, not blocked: proxies do not simply stratify observed confounders; they impose bridge equations across latent cells.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from stratified bounds with partially observed confounders.
seed_details.rebuttal: Proxy bridge constraints are moment equations, not observed strata, and they interact with the missing mediator joint law.

proposed_from.literature_map: RobinsGreenland1992 and Pearl2001 define direct/natural effects; ImaiKeeleYamamoto2010 and TchetgenShpitser2012 settle single-mediator identification/efficiency under strong ignorability; DanielDeStavolaCousensVansteelandt2015 and VanderWeeleVansteelandt2014 show multiple mediators require joint reasoning; VansteelandtDaniel2017 uses interventional mediator draws and highlights the joint mediator distribution as an estimand input; XiaChan2022 uses copula-compatible nuisance models for multiple natural mediation effects; DingVanderWeele2016 and MilesKankiMeloniTchetgen2017 give single-mediator sensitivity/partial-ID bounds under mediator-outcome confounding; DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 gives proximal point identification for natural mediation effects; GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 gives proxy partial-ID bounds without completeness; GabrielSachsSjolander2023 gives sharp two-binary-mediator decomposition bounds and warns separately sharp component bounds need not combine; HanLuoZhangGu2026 gives recent bounds when part of mediator-endpoint confounding is observed. The open tension used here is that the closest multi-mediator sharp-bounds paper assumes joint mediator information, while the closest proxy papers do not handle an unknown mediator coupling in a natural-effect target.
